answer:
Several works in the corpus mention **gardens** (often poetically, as symbols of existence, love, paradise, or transience in Urdu/Persianate poetry, or literally as property in Dharmaśāstras) or the **gardener** (sometimes as a metaphorical self-image for the poet or a figure of cultivation/patience, or literally in economic/agricultural contexts). No single work develops an extended allegorical "figure" akin to, say, the biblical Edenic gardener, but the motif recurs thematically.

### Poetic/Metaphorical Uses (Primary Matches for "Figure")
- **Bang-E-Dara Part 1** (Allama Iqbal): Gardens abound as metaphors for being, loss, and desire (e.g., "This garden of Being," "my garden," "love's own garden"); gardener appears as "God made no gardener needful unto me" (an angel speaking as a self-sufficient desert tree).[^1][^2]
- **Bang-E-Dara Part 2** (Allama Iqbal): Poet as gardener—"I am its gardener, Love its season's prime"—of a metaphorical "garden of verse."[^3]
- **Bang-E-Dara Part 3** (Allama Iqbal): Direct address to a gardener amid a blighted garden (symbolizing despair, perhaps national renewal): "Do not despair, O gardener, at the skies’ / Dark hue, or at the garden’s present plight."[^4]
- **Diwan-E-Ghalib** (Mirza Ghalib): Gardens as fleeting beauty or contrast to worldly markets (e.g., "Why would the rose leave gardens for the crowded mart?").[^5]

### Literal or Contextual Uses (Economic/Legal)
- **Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II** (Charles Dunoyer): Gardeners referenced in agriculture; ideal loam soil "which gardeners regard as the most proper for vegetation."[^6]
- **Traité de Législation: VOL IV** (Charles Comte): Slaves lack "the intelligence and activity necessary for a gardener"; contrasts crude farming with skilled gardening.[^7]
- **Bṛhaspati Smṛti**: Gardens as seizable property in disputes ("Fields, gardens, houses, and so on").[^8]
- Other Dharmaśāstras (**Kātyāyana Smṛti**, **Manusmṛti**, **Nāradasmṛti**) similarly treat gardens literally as boundaries/property in legal contexts (e.g., disputes over "fields, gardens, houses").[^9]

Minor mentions (e.g., "gardening" in **Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I**, gardens in **Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana**) exist but are peripheral. No other works match.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Ch. 23 "Farishte" [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/23-farishte/translation/#p-365211).
[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Ch. 3 "Gulzar-e-hast-o-bud na begana-waar dekh" [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/03-gulzar-e-hast-o-bud-na-begana-waar-dekh/translation/#p-5c815a).
[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, Ch. 22 "Sitara" [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/22-sitara/translation/#p-0844ec).
[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Ch. 4 "Dil se jo baat nikalti hai asar rakhti hai" [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/04-dil-se-jo-baat-nikalti-hai-asar-rakhti-hai/translation/#p-169e10).
[^5]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, Ch. 92 "Jis bazm mein tu naaz se guftaar mein aave" [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/92-jis-bazm-mein-tu-naaz-se-guftaar-mein-aave/translation/#p-a28332).
[^6]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, Ch. 17 "VII. De la liberté de l'industrie agricole [ 130 ]" [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/17-vii-de-la-liberte-de-lindustrie-agricole-130/translation/#p-8b8cfe).
[^7]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL IV*, Ch. 13 "De l’influence de l’esclavage domestique..." [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-iv-bca040/13-de-l-influence-de-l-esclavage-domestique-sur-la-pr/translation/#p-d243a4).
[^8]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Ch. 1 [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-c5ca69).
[^9]: E.g., *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, Ch. 59 [paragraph](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/59/translation/#p-825353).
